summary: Target → Emit → Filter → Atlas → Test-plan → Eval → Insert → Promote.

stages.label: Target
stages.purpose: Allocate exploration pressure across archive cells, bridge regions, and themes.

stages.label: Emit
stages.purpose: Generate candidate cards under explicit constraints and targeting.

stages.label: Filter
stages.purpose: Apply risk gates and interestingness routing without admission authority.

stages.label: Atlas
stages.purpose: Extract mechanisms, causal triples, and neighborhood context for comparatives.

stages.label: Test-plan
stages.purpose: Build falsification ladders and schedule cheapest useful probes.

stages.label: Eval
stages.purpose: Evaluate against incumbent, neighbor, and null baselines; mint evidence objects.

stages.label: Insert
stages.purpose: Update append-only archive mechanically; record breakthrough events.

stages.label: Promote
stages.purpose: Rank promotions by mechanical scoring and emit queue artifacts.

invariant: Every stage produces schema-bound artifacts; failures repair or hard-stop with explicit error objects; no silent corruption.
